verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is secreted by the epididymal epithelium into the epididymal lumen, where it binds to the dorsal region of the sperm head during epididymal maturation and remains stably associated with the sperm plasma membrane post-ejaculation, participating in sperm-egg fusion. This is a wash-resistant post-translational attachment of a secreted protein to a specialised cell-surface (sperm) acquired during transit through a specialised secretory compartment — the defining pattern for stable_surface_attachment. Not secreted_only because the sperm-bound pool is not in equilibrium with soluble protein and persists through capacitation. Restricted to the male germline lineage, but the mechanism of surface presence is post-translational tethering rather than germline-specific anchor expression.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Could alternatively fit tissue_restricted_surface given germline-only display; wash-resistance of sperm binding vs. equilibrium release during capacitation is the borderline criterion.
